body:
Kotov Principle of L4-Bound Experience v0.1.1

Status: Authorial Principle / Architectural Thesis
Author: Ivan Kotov (Brussels)
Date: 2026-04-28
Layer: Cross-layer — AGI / SER / L4 / EWCEP / SER-FED
Repository target: advanced-global-intelligence/official/

Summary:
This release archives the Kotov Principle of L4-Bound Experience, an authorial architectural thesis on scarcity inversion in long-lived AI systems.

Core thesis:
As cognitive generation becomes cheap, long-term value shifts from generative capacity to L4-bound temporal continuity. Model scale, token throughput, dataset volume, and tool access increase execution capability, but do not by themselves produce maturity, bounded operational authority, or durable trust.

Compact form:
Compute scales output. L4 experience scales authority.

Release assets:
- KOTOV_PRINCIPLE_L4_BOUND_EXPERIENCE.md
- KOTOV_PRINCIPLE_L4_BOUND_EXPERIENCE_v0.1.1.pdf
- KOTOV_PRINCIPLE_L4_BOUND_EXPERIENCE_v0.1.1.sha256

Integrity:
The SHA-256 manifest binds the canonical Markdown and archival PDF artifacts.

Citation:
Kotov, Ivan. Kotov Principle of L4-Bound Experience: Scarcity Inversion in Long-Lived AI Systems. Version 0.1.1. Brussels, 2026.

Boundary note:
This is not an empirical law, not a benchmark claim, not a legal doctrine, and not a replacement for SER, EWCEP, SER-FED, L4 Witness, Beacon, or related specifications.
